assumptions: No Dropbox app or access token exists.
A terminal with cURL is available.

developer_goal: Create a Dropbox app, generate an access token, and list the root folder with cURL.

first_success.outcome: Authenticated cURL POST to /2/files/list_folder returns folder listing JSON for the authorized account.
first_success.observable_signal: HTTP 200 JSON includes entries (possibly empty) for path "".

route_selection.surface: Dropbox generated access token + cURL files/list_folder
route_selection.rule: Measure documented developer onboarding: account creation through first success. Prefer the vendor quickstart or hosted API/console path when one exists.
route_selection.selected: Dropbox generated access token + cURL files/list_folder

candidates.name: Dropbox generated access token + cURL files/list_folder
candidates.reason: Dropbox OAuth guide recommends a generated access token for quick own-account API testing before implementing OAuth. HTTP docs demonstrate cURL POST to https://api.dropboxapi.com/2/files/list_folder with Authorization Bearer.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to Dropbox and open the App Console.
Create an app with the required permissions for files.list_folder.
In OAuth 2 settings, click Generate and store the access token.
required_path.observable_result: Token value available for Bearer auth.

required_path.action: POST https://api.dropboxapi.com/2/files/list_folder with Authorization Bearer and JSON {"path": ""}.

prerequisites.description: Dropbox account and App Console app.
Generated OAuth 2 access token for own account.

excluded.item: OAuth code flow for other users

uncertainties.question: Are all signup and first-success fields for Dropbox fully enumerated in public docs?
uncertainties.impact: Atomic field counts inside account or billing steps may be incomplete.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Live console fields can differ from documentation screenshots.
